cases.description: Default return_type 1: Sunday=1..Saturday=7. Feb 14, 2008 is a Thursday. Source: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/weekday-function-60e44483-2ed1-439f-8bd0-e404c190949a (worked example, WEEKDAY(A2)=5)
return_type 2: Monday=1..Sunday=7. Source: Microsoft WEEKDAY docs worked example, WEEKDAY(A2,2)=4.
return_type 3: Monday=0..Sunday=6 (zero-based). Thursday is 3 in this scheme.
cases.expected_note: Per Microsoft's return_type table, mode 3 maps Monday..Sunday to 0..6; Thursday (the 4th weekday counting Monday=0) = 3.

cases.description: return_type 12: Tuesday=1..Monday=7 (ISO-style variant start day). Thursday is the 3rd day in this scheme.
cases.expected_note: Per Microsoft's return_type table, mode 12 maps Tuesday=1,Wed=2,Thu=3,...,Monday=7.

cases.description: return_type 16: Saturday=1..Friday=7. Thursday is the 6th day in this scheme.
cases.expected_note: Per Microsoft's return_type table, mode 16 maps Saturday=1,Sun=2,Mon=3,Tue=4,Wed=5,Thu=6,Fri=7.

cases.description: An out-of-range return_type (not one of the documented 1,2,3,11-17 values) is a documented error condition.
cases.expected_note: Microsoft docs: an invalid return_type raises #NUM!.
